variables.credibility: Words — can we be believed? Expertise, accuracy, knowledge.
variables.reliability: Actions — do we meet expectations? Follow-through, consistency, deadlines.
variables.intimacy: Emotions — do they feel safe? Confidentiality, genuine care, vulnerability.
variables.self_orientation: Focus — is motivation self-serving or other-centered? THE DENOMINATOR. Most powerful variable.

steps: Engage — initiate dialogue, help clients acknowledge needs, develop credibility
Listen — create space for emotional dimensions, practice deep understanding
Frame — offer fresh perspectives, state root issues clearly, show creative insight
Envision — guide toward desired future states, sketch alternate realities
Commit — establish concrete actions, agree on steps, renew commitment energy

quadrants: Expert for Hire — starting point, reactive, narrow
Trusted Advisor — individual trust earned, but narrow firm relationship
Vendor — broad revenue but transactional, RFP territory
Trusted Client Partnership — both advisory status AND firm-wide capability

hacks: Focus on the person, not the problem
Self-confidence — ask 'What value do I bring beyond solving this?'
Ego strength — mantra: 'It's not about me'
Inclusive professionalism — 'We will be more successful together'
The relationship is the goal — relationship > any single transaction

guidelines: Passion — commit deeply to work you genuinely care about
People — understand how humans actually function

three_pillars: Functional Expertise — deep specialized knowledge enabling confident execution
Objective Analysis — third-party perspective to counteract internal biases
On-Demand Resources — specialized talent when internal teams lack capacity
mckinsey_differentiation.positioning: Embedded partnership at multiple levels, adapting role based on client needs. Advisory relationships, not one-off service engagements.

key_quotes.maister_professionalism: Act like a true professional, aiming for true excellence, and the money will follow. Act like a prostitute, with an attitude of 'I'll do it for the money,' and you'll lose the premium that excellence earns.
key_quotes.maister_philosophy: My philosophy of business, and life itself, is that you earn and deserve what you want to get back from the world by giving first.
key_quotes.green_core: The way to be a great advisor is to care about your client.
key_quotes.green_helpfulness: It is not enough to be right, you must also be helpful.
key_quotes.self_orientation: A person with low self-orientation breaks through skepticism because their focus is crystal clear: your success, not theirs.
key_quotes.denominator_principle: Your trustworthiness plummets the moment you prioritize your interests over others'.
key_quotes.strategy_fat_smoker: Organizations typically know what they should do — the challenge lies in consistent execution despite short-term temptations.

trusted_advisor_characteristics: Strong ego control — subordinate own ego
Prioritize problem definition over technical mastery
Internalized drive for doing right things
Willingness to take personal risks with clients

advisor_behaviors: Focused on client outcomes and relationships
Invited to the table — no RFP needed
